title: Sagenia polymorpha
taxonomy: Sagenia polymorpha is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Sagenia and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Sagenia polymorpha is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. Within the family Thelypteridaceae, Sagenia polymorpha is closely related to other species such as Sagenia australis and Sagenia truncata. Sagenia polymorpha is often found growing in shaded and humid environments, where it can thrive as an epiphyte or terrestrial plant.

intro: Sagenia polymorpha, also known as Sagenia, is a plant species belonging to the fern family (Dryopteridaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sagenia polymorpha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
